title: AX Designer — Agent Experience
companyDescription: Meridian Health is a FAANG-adjacent health tech company at a major tech campus. We build AI-powered diagnostic and patient engagement tools. 2,000+ employees, publicly traded.
description:
Meridian Health is building AI tools that help patients manage chronic conditions and doctors make faster, more accurate diagnoses. Our agents don't just answer questions — they analyze patient data, suggest treatment pathways, and proactively alert clinicians to potential issues.

We're creating a new role: AX Designer. This person will design how doctors, nurses, and patients interact with our AI diagnostic agent. You'll define the agent's behavior, design the conversation model, create trust signals for high-stakes medical decisions, and ensure every interaction meets the highest accessibility standards.

This is a unique opportunity to define the AX discipline at scale. You'll work with our clinical advisors, ML team, and design leadership to create standards for AI interaction design that will influence products used by millions of patients.
responsibilities: Design the interaction model for our AI diagnostic agent, including how it presents findings, handles uncertainty, and escalates to human review
Define agent behavior specifications: decision trees, confidence thresholds, and escalation rules
Create conversation flows that help clinicians understand and trust the agent's recommendations
Design trust signals appropriate for healthcare: transparency indicators, explanation formats, and audit trails
Conduct usability research with clinicians to validate agent designs and iterate based on feedback
Develop and document AX design patterns for our product team
Ensure all AI interactions meet WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility standards
requirements: 4+ years of UX design experience, with exposure to AI/ML product design
Strong understanding of conversation design and interaction design principles
Experience designing for high-stakes or regulated environments (healthcare, finance, legal)
Portfolio demonstrating complex system design and interaction modeling
Understanding of accessibility requirements and inclusive design practices
Ability to collaborate with non-design stakeholders (clinicians, ML engineers, product managers)
Proficiency in Figma, prototyping tools, and design documentation
niceToHave: Experience in healthcare or life sciences design
Background in clinical or medical workflow design
Understanding of how AI/ML models make decisions and their limitations
Experience with design systems at enterprise scale
Publications or presentations on AI interaction design
